zh.lifeSummary: 當Baron Robert de Luttrell - Third Baron Ingham於1255年出生在英國英格蘭林可爾史爾英厄姆時，他的父親Baron Geoffrey de Luttrell II20歲，母親Joan de Grey24歲。他於大約1274年與Joan de Taillebois在英國英格蘭越可爾胡頓 帕格爾結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和6個女兒的父母。他去世於1297年6月在他的家鄉享年42歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Baron Robert de Luttrell - Third Baron Ingham于1255年出生在英国英格兰林可爾史爾英厄姆时，他的父亲Baron Geoffrey de Luttrell II20岁，母亲Joan de Grey24岁。他于大约1274年与Joan de Taillebois在英国英格兰越可爾胡頓 帕格爾结婚。他们至少是3个儿子和6个女儿的父母。他去世于1297年6月在他的家乡享年42岁。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Luttrell
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Irish (of Norman origin): from a diminutive of Old French loutre ‘otter’ (from Latin lutra), applied as a nickname for someone thought to resemble an otter, or a metonymic occupational name for someone who hunted otters (for their pelts). Compare Luter and Litteral .

variants.name: Luttrull
meanings: English: variant of   Luttrell  .

variants.name: Littrell
meanings: English: variant of   Luttrell  .

variants.name: Littrel
meanings: English: variant of   Luttrell  .

variants.name: Litteral
meanings: Altered form of English   Luttrell  .

variants.name: Luter
meanings: from Old French loutre ‘otter’, either used as a nickname, or alternatively as a metonymic occupational name to denote an otter hunter.
variant of   Luther   2.
English:

nameMeanings.name: Third
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: Scottish: locative name from one or more of various minor places so called because they were at one time farms divided among three tenants.

nameMeanings.name: Baron
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: French, English, Dutch, Polish, Czech, Romanian, and Croatian; Breton (also Le Baron): from Old French barun, baron, Middle English barun, baroun, Middle Dutch baroen, Polish, Czech, Romanian, and dialectal Croatian baron ‘baron’ (i.e. a nobleman ranking below an earl and above a knight), a title of nobility which was in England also awarded to certain freemen of the cities of London and York and of the Cinque Ports. As a surname it is unlikely to be a status name denoting a person of rank. Generally, it referred to service in a baronial household or was acquired as a nickname by a peasant who had ideas above his station or applied to a man showing characteristics of a nobleman. See also 3 and 4 below, compare Barron and Lebaron .
French: habitational name from (Le) Baron, the name of several places in various parts of France. Compare   Lebaron  .
German: status name for a freeman or baron, from barūn ‘imperial or church official’, a loanword in Middle High German from Old French (see 1 above).

variants.name: Barron
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Juan, Jesus, Manuel, Francisco, Carlos, Miguel, Raul, Pedro, Ruben, Alfredo, Luis.

Scottish: status name from Scots baron, which in the Highlands denoted a small proprietary landholder. In Angus it alluded specifically to a tenant of one of the small baronies attached to the Abbey of Coupar Angus. The surname was taken by Scottish migrants to Ulster. Compare Baron .
Scottish: shortened form of McBarron, from Scottish Gaelic Mac a' Bharain ‘son of the baron’. See Baron and 1 above.

variants.name: Boron
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Polish Krystyna, Edyta, Leszek, Mieczyslaw, Mieczyslawa, Wladyslaw.
Polish (Boroń): from a pet form of the personal names Borzysław or Borzymir, formed with the element bor ‘to fight’ (from Old Slavic boriti se).
French: habitational name from a place so called in Franche-Comté.

variants.name: Baro
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Mario, Manuel, Ana, Angel, Carlos, Cesar, Fernando, Jesus, Jose, Orlando, Ramon, Rosalia.  Italian Guido, Angelo, Carmelo, Federico, Lucio.
Catalan (Baró): status name from baró ‘free man, baron’ (see Baron 1), or a habitational name from a place so named.
Hungarian (Báró): status name from báró ‘baron’. In the form Baró, it could be from a pet form of the personal names Barabás (see Barabas ) or Bertalan.

variants.name: Barona
meanings: Spanish: from the feminine form of Barón ‘baron’ (see Baron ), applied as a nickname or as an occupational name for a member of the household of a baroness.
Italian: probably from the feminine form of   Barone  . The name is concentrated in Sondrio province (Lombardy).

variants.name: Barren
meanings: English: probably a variant of   Baron   or   Barran  .

nameMeanings.name: Ingham
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Yorkshire and Lancashire): habitational name from any of the places called Ingham, particularly in Lincolnshire, Norfolk, and Suffolk. The placename is from the Old English personal name Inga + hām ‘homestead’. In Yorkshire this name was sometimes confused with Bingham in the 18th century.
English (of Norman origin): variant of the obsolete Norman surname Engaine; see Gain .

variants.name: Bingham
meanings: English (Dorset) and Irish (County Mayo): habitational name from Bingham (Nottinghamshire). The placename is probably from an Old English folk-name Bynningas (‘the people associated with a man named Bynna’), or possibly from an unattested Old English word bing ‘a kettle-shaped hollow’, + Old English hām ‘homestead’.
Irish (Ulster, of Scottish origin): altered form of   Bigham  .
American shortened and altered form of various like-sounding Jewish surnames such as   Bingenheimer  .

variants.name: Gain
meanings: English and Scottish (of Norman origin): nickname for a cunning person, from Engaine, which is based on Old French engaigne ‘ingenuity, cunning, trickery’.

English: habitiational name from any of various places called after the Engain family, including Colne Engaine and Gaynes Park (Essex), d'Engaine's Farm (Cambridgeshire), and Aston Ingham (Hereford). Engaine (see 1 above) was the name of a powerful Norman family with lands in several counties. The original form of the surname has since died out, surviving as Gain and its variants. There has also been considerable confusion with Ingham .
French: nickname from a noun derivative of Old French gaainier ‘to obtain, gain, win’.

nameMeanings.name: Robert
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the many French names of Germanic origin that were introduced into Britain by the Normans; it has since remained in continuous use. It is derived from the nearly synonymous elements hrōd ‘fame’ + berht ‘bright, famous’, and had a native Old English predecessor of similar form (Hreodbeorht), which was supplanted by the Norman name. Two dukes of Normandy in the 11th century bore the name: the father of William the Conqueror (sometimes identified with the legendary Robert the Devil), and his eldest son. It was borne also by three kings of Scotland, notably Robert the Bruce ( 1274–1329 ), who freed Scotland from English domination. The altered short form Bob is very common, but Hob and Dob, which were common in the Middle Ages and gave rise to surnames, are extinct. See also Rupert .

en.lifeSummary: When Baron Robert de Luttrell - Third Baron Ingham was born in 1255, in Ingham, Lincolnshire, England, United Kingdom, his father, Baron Geoffrey de Luttrell II, was 20 and his mother, Joan de Grey, was 24. He married Joan de Taillebois about 1274, in Hooton Pagnell, Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 6 daughters. He died in June 1297, in his hometown, at the age of 42.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Baron Robert de Luttrell - Third Baron Ingham est né en 1255, à Ingham, Lincolnshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Baron Geoffrey de Luttrell II, avait 20 ans et sa mère, Joan de Grey, avait 24 ans. Il a épousé Joan de Taillebois environ 1274, à Hooton Pagnell, Everwyck, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 6 filles. Il est décédé en juin 1297, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 42 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Baron Robert de Luttrell - Third Baron Ingham 1255 in Ingham, Lincolnshire, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war sein Vater Baron Geoffrey de Luttrell II 20 und seine Mutter Joan de Grey 24. Er hat um 1274 in Hooton Pagnell, Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom Joan de Taillebois geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne und 6 Töchter. Er starb im Juni 1297 im Alter von 42 Jahren in seinem Geburtsort.

Lui sposato Joan de Taillebois circa 1274, nel Hooton Pagnell, Yorkshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli e 6 figlie. Morì nel giugno 1297, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 42 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Baron Robert de Luttrell - Third Baron Inghamが1255年にイギリスイギリスリンコルンシュアイアインガムで生まれた時，父Baron Geoffrey de Luttrell IIは20歳，母Joan de Greyは24歳であった。約1274年にイギリスイギリスヨークシュアイアホーオトン プアグネルでJoan de Tailleboisと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子と6人の娘の親であった。彼は1297年6月に故郷で42歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Baron Robert de Luttrell - Third Baron Ingham(이)가 1255년 영국 잉글랜드 링코실르 잉함에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Baron Geoffrey de Luttrell II(은)는 20세였고, 그의 어머니 Joan de Grey(은)는 24세였다. 그는 대략 1274년 영국 잉글랜드 요르크실르 후턴 파그넬에서 Joan de Taillebois(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 여섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1297년 6월 그의 고향에서 42세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Baron Robert de Luttrell - Third Baron Ingham родился в 1255 г. в местности ингхам, линколншир, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени Baron Geoffrey de Luttrell II было 20 лет, а матери, которую звали Joan de Grey, 24. Он женился на женщине по имени Joan de Taillebois около 1274 г. в местности хутон пагнелл, еркшир, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 6 дочерей. Он умер июнь 1297 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 42 лет.
